items.title: Labor’s skilled migration program exposed as less than half of ‘skilled’ visa recipients are primary workers - Sky News Australia
items.commentary: Analysis of the skilled migration program found that fewer than half of skilled visa recipients are primary applicants, with the remainder being accompanying family members. This affects how the composition of skilled migration intake is understood by prospective applicants and employers relying on the program.

items.title: 'I'm astounded': The visa fee that's nearly tripled in price - SBS
items.commentary: A particular Australian visa fee has nearly tripled in price, prompting concern from affected applicants. People applying under this category now face substantially higher upfront costs when lodging their applications.

items.title: Australia raises salary requirement for skilled work visas - MSN
items.commentary: Australia has raised the minimum salary requirement for skilled work visas. Employers sponsoring workers and applicants must now meet a higher income threshold to qualify under skilled visa pathways.

items.title: The overlooked factor in Australia's migration debate that's keeping figures high - SBS
items.commentary: An examination of Australia's migration debate highlights an often-overlooked factor keeping net migration figures elevated. This context helps explain why overall migration numbers remain high despite policy adjustments.

items.title: 'Robbing people using visas': Applicants shocked by sudden price hike - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.commentary: Visa applicants reported shock at sudden increases in application charges, describing the rises as excessive. Those planning to apply across affected visa categories now face significantly higher fees.

items.title: From student and TR visas to partner and PR: Australian visa fees hikes across most categories - SBS
items.commentary: Australian visa application fees increased across most categories, including student, temporary resident, partner and permanent residency visas. Applicants across nearly all visa types now face higher costs when lodging applications.

items.title: New financial year and migration: 'Focus on complying with your visa conditions' - SBS
items.commentary: With the start of the new financial year, authorities emphasised the importance of complying with visa conditions. Visa holders are reminded to ensure they meet their obligations to avoid breaches that could affect their status.

items.title: Surprise hike in international student visa application fees “a direct hit to Australia’s competitiveness” - ICEF Monitor
items.commentary: International student visa application fees rose unexpectedly, raising concerns about Australia's competitiveness as a study destination. Prospective international students now face higher upfront costs to apply for a student visa.

items.title: Outrageous 25-200% Australian visa fee hike hits international students, partners and skilled workers from 1 July - The Australia Today
items.commentary: Visa application fees increased by between 25 and 200 per cent from 1 July, affecting international students, partners and skilled workers. Applicants across these categories must now pay substantially more to lodge their applications.

items.title: Australia's 2026 migration changes: what's law, what's only proposed, and what it means for you - NRI Affairs
items.commentary: An overview clarified which of Australia's 2026 migration changes are now law and which remain only proposed. This distinction helps applicants understand which rules currently apply and which may change in future.

items.title: Skilled visa invites reveal Australia's workforce priorities - Independent Australia
items.commentary: Recent skilled visa invitation rounds revealed which occupations and workforce sectors Australia is prioritising. Prospective skilled applicants can use this to gauge their prospects in current selection rounds.

items.title: Migration dips as Coalition accuses government of failing to meet targets - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
items.commentary: Migration figures declined, with the opposition claiming the government failed to meet its stated targets. Shifts in overall migration levels can influence future visa planning and intake settings.

items.title: Labor denies spousal visa delays being used to reduce migration figures - Sky News Australia
items.commentary: The government denied that spousal visa processing delays were being used to reduce overall migration figures. Partner visa applicants continue to experience uncertainty about processing timeframes.

items.title: The long wait for permanency in Australia's shrinking humanitarian program - SBS
items.commentary: A report examined the extended wait for permanent status within Australia's shrinking humanitarian program. Refugees and humanitarian entrants face prolonged uncertainty before securing permanent residency.

items.title: ‘Ridiculous’: Aussie’s $11k visa disaster - News.com.au
items.commentary: An applicant described an $11,000 visa cost as a financial disaster amid rising fees. The report illustrates the significant expense some individuals face when navigating the Australian visa system.
